segments.text: Why did Fruity stop looking like a juice
box and started looking like a hazardous
chemical? This was not a mistake. It was
a [music] calculated move to save the
brand from dying. See, for 30 years,
Fruity was that cute, nostalgic [music]
green, yellow rectangle. But nostalgia
does not sell forever. It felt old and
on a crowded shelf, [music] it was
becoming invisible. So they hired
Pentagram, a world famous design agency,
to burn their past. They dished the box
and created a new bottle shape inspired
by a totem pole. But a cool shape was
not enough. The biggest problem with
selling juice is the shelf. When a
bottle sits on a rack, a horizontal logo
is tiny [music] and you can barely read
it from a distance. So they did
something radical. They rotated the logo
by 90°. By going vertical, [music] the
logo now takes up the entire height of
the bottle and it screams at you from
far away. So now it's not just
packaging, it's a billboard in your
